document_metadata.project_name: BLCS Main Pool Hall Heating & Ventilation - AHU 1 & 3 Refurbishments

aim.intro: The Request for Tender (RFT) is for the BLCS Main Pool Hall Heating & Ventilation - AHU 1 & 3 Refurbishments to Council for the service of procurement, installation, and integration of specified works. The tender period spans over a duration agreed upon in context.
aim.body: This Tender Evaluation and Probity Plan (TEPP) is the planning and control document in conducting the evaluation of tenders received in response to RFT. It sets out: • processes and principles for evaluating tenders; • individual's responsibilities, including ensuring all work complies with BCC WHS requirements and proper crane usage approvals are obtained from Council before hoisting equipment; • the evaluation schedule based on a detailed program submitted by tenderer to minimize disruption during peak hours of leisure centre operation (6:00am - 9:00pm daily); and • reporting requirements, including making good all disturbed work surfaces as per context.

description_of_requirement: The BLCS Main Pool Hall Heating & Ventilation - AHU 1 & 3 Refurbishments involve the comprehensive upgrade of existing heating, ventilation and air conditioning systems within a leisure centre's main pool hall. This includes replacing outdated equipment with new reverse cycle water-cooled heat pump chillers to enhance energy efficiency.

The refurbishment also encompasses the installation of additional plant room external wall louvers for better access during delivery and retrieval processes, as well as making good any disturbances caused by these activities.

General:
• All work must be carried out under a Council TEPP with an Electrical Contractors license.
• The installation of the new chiller systems shall comply with AS1677.2 and any other relevant SAA publications covering design, manufacture or installation requirements for electrical equipment in accordance with local Electricity Supply Authority regulations.
• Licensed Personnel Only access to switchboards is mandatory where all work must be carried out by suitably qualified tradespersons experienced in the relevant trade and compliant with WorkSafe NSW regulations and Council WHS requirements.
• The Contractor shall provide a detailed schedule showing proposed sequences of works, durations for which various areas will need to be available, considering leisure centre's operational hours (6:00am - 9:00pm daily) with minimal disruption during peak times and ensuring that noisy work is limited or scheduled outside typical working hours.
• All materials used in the refurbishment must comply with relevant Australian Standards, including those for electrical installations (AS/IEC standards), mechanical services, air & water systems, wiring diagrams, fire safety measures such as intumescent fittings where penetrating a fire wall is required.
technical_requirements:
Electrical Installation:
• All equipment supplied and work done shall comply with AS/NZS Wiring Rules, local Electricity Supply Authority requirements, SAA publications on design, manufacture or installation of electrical equipment.
• Use the latest issue or amendment of relevant S.A.A publication for switchboard conformity and type test certificate from a qualified testing laboratory as proof.
• Switchboards shall comply with AS 61439 standards; ensure performance is substantiated by manufacturers' submission of a type test certificate.
• Ensure all cables not enclosed are neatly arranged, tied and supported. Power cabling terminations to switchboard must terminate at an approved terminal block without exceeding two cable termination points per point.
• Direct power connections for Compressor and Condenser motors allowed only if specified in the design drawings.
• All wiring penetrating a fire wall shall be installed using special intumescent fittings as required by AS3000 standards.
• Conduct all testing, commissioning including visual inspections for leaks and integrity of controls cabling. Sequence checks on specified control functions must also be carried out before requesting Council attendance at the site.
• Extend existing Building Management Control System to include new works with identical logic as AHU 2 refurbishments, ensuring BMCS integration for complete system operation and commissioning within Defects Liability Period without additional cost.

Switchboard Upgrade:
• Provide a plinth with SS Tray under the feet of each closed system.
• Install drainage to waste for each closed system on provided corrosion coupons, tested every three months as per AS/NZS standards and local Electricity Supply Authority requirements.
• Ensure all equipment supplied and work done complies with the specification, S.A.A Wiring Rules (AS 3110), latest issue or amendment used for switchboards in accordance to AS/NZS standards as per Council's Electrical Design Guide.
• Switchboard manufacturer must provide a type test certificate from an accredited testing laboratory, and the installation contractor should have relevant licenses with only certified electricians employed on-site for field wiring & cabling installations following AS/NZS standards (AS 3000).
• Label 'Authorised Personnel Only' section to prevent contact with live parts, and label all other sections as indicated in switchboard drawings.
• Ensure LED pilot light indicators for main components are provided along with individual lamp test button. Individual fault indicator lights must be installed for safety controls.

Commissioning of Electrical Works:
• All equipment supplied and work done shall comply with AS/NZS standards, S.A.A Wiring Rules, local Electricity Supply Authority requirements.
• Switchboards to conform with AS 61439; submit type test certificate from a qualified testing laboratory for the switchboard manufacturer's submission.
• Ensure programming of work and subsequent site management minimize disruption during leisure centre's activities, based on provided schedule considering opening hours (6:00am to 9:00pm).
• All wiring penetrating a fire wall must use special fire rated intumescent fittings.
• Provide all necessary resources for testing and commissioning before Council inspections, with fully detailed Test Log sheets A4 in size presenting clear records of tests to be carried out.
• Visual inspection of pipework joints; water-test drains & overflow piping for leaks; check power cabling terminations using megger test each circuit.
• Verify integrity and continuity of all controls cabling, ensuring electrical connections are free from shorts and grounds. Sequence checks of control functions such as Start, Stop, Chiller Staging cycles to be carried out by the Contractor.
• Extend existing Building Management Control System (BMCS) for new works with identical logic; supply necessary additional points for controlling new equipment; re-engineer and program BMCS accordingly. Integrate a HVAC and BMCS Chiller BACnet High Level Interface gateway, carry out all programming to ensure current strategy is maintained.
• Install LED pilot light 'Run' & 'Fault' indication for main components including supply/exhaust fans; individual lamp test button required. Individual pilot light fault indicator provided for safety controls as well.
• All cables not enclosed shall be neatly arranged and tied, with power cables terminating at approved terminal blocks no more than two per point unless otherwise specified in the drawings or scope of works.
• Supply 7.5kW ABB ACH580 VSD's to respective circulating pumps for heat recovery coils; ensure all other electrical, hydraulics and associated works comply with current codes and safe operation systems as per the provided drawings or scope of work.
• Remove external wall louvre upon delivery/retrieval of equipment. Dispose off-site any job generated rubbish including redundant equipment; coordinate access to centre's miscellaneous stocks needed for its operations, allowing pool staff safe movement through the working area during installation and commissioning phases.
• Allow progressive fine-tuning of control logic after Commissioning up to end of Defects Liability Period without additional cost. Ensure all equipment is removed from site post completion unless otherwise specified in tender documents or scope of works, leaving a tidy condition for Council inspection and acceptance.

Maintenance Schedule for Electrical Systems:
• Immediate submission of detailed work schedule to Council post-award.
• Working hours limited to typical leisure centre operation times or agreed upon with management, especially during noisy works.
• Minimum notice period of 48 hours for electrical shutdowns required by written approval from the council officer in charge and center's management.
• Ensure all equipment supplied complies strictly with AS/NZS standards, SafeWork NSW legislation, relevant local authority requirements, including S.A.A Wiring Rules for electrical installations.
• Switchboards to conform with the latest edition of AS 61439 and possess a type test certificate from an accredited testing laboratory as proof of performance.
• Employ only licensed electricians on-site, ensuring all switchboard manufacturers provide necessary certifications for their products' design, manufacture, and performance.
• All connections to be neatly arranged with control cables terminating at approved terminal blocks; no more than two cables per point except where specified otherwise (e.g., direct termination on motors).
• Installation of 7.5kW ABB ACH580 VSDs for circulating pumps in heat recovery coils.
• Co-ordination with center management and council officer regarding access to equipment delivery, retrieval of redundant equipment, rubbish disposal offsite, movement through work areas by pool staffs, ensuring minimal disruption during the refurbishment process.
• Extension of existing Building Management Control System (BMCS) for additional control points as required and integration with new chiller BACnet High Level Interface gateway without extra cost beyond Defects Liability Period. Testing and commissioning to be completed before full operation, allowing fine-tuning post-commissioning.
• Hoisting strategy proposal submission by the contractor for approval prior to any hoisting activities; no lifting unless written consent is provided from Council or center's management.
• All disturbed work and damage resulting therefrom must be made good in full satisfaction of council, including clean-up post completion. Removal of all debris after works are completed.
invoicing: Invoices must be itemised and reference purchase order and contract number
Standard 30 day payment terms from receipt of valid tax invoice
Milestone/progress claims to align with agreed program and deliverables
Variations require prior written approval by Council

Monthly summary including KPI performance and incidents
Final report pack including commissioning results and certificates
Provide as-built drawings, O&M manuals and warranties

performance_monitoring.note: The tender document outlines the comprehensive requirements for a refurbishment project at Blacktown Leisure Centre's Main Pool Hall Heating & Ventilation AHU systems, specifically focusing on BLCS-001 TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS. The contractor is responsible for ensuring all wiring penetrates the fire wall with specialized intumescent fittings and must submit detailed test logs prior to testing.
items: Quality of workmanship and defect rate
Compliance with WHS and environmental obligations

desired_outcomes: Strong WHS culture with zero harm
Value for money over the whole-of-life
purpose_rft: Set out the requirements, terms and evaluation method for the procurement
Identify either a single supplier or a panel arrangement as permitted by legislation
Achieve best value consistent with Council policies and probity

risks.action: FAT; point-to-point I/O test; SAT with witness
Verify duty; commissioning with TAB report

evaluation_schedule.task: Review Testing & Commissioning plan and witness tests
Review maintenance schedules and O&M handover
Review BMCS HLI/point list and integration test records

tender_evaluation.methodology_text: The Evaluation Committee, in accordance with the methodology specified in the RFT and reproduced here, will evaluate all tenders.

required_criteria_table.rationale: Given the high commissioning complexity and safety critical nature of this project with a warranty period extending up to one year, it's crucial that cost does not exceed our budget constraints. Therefore, we allocate more weight here

With 8 EC fans in operation requiring specialized knowledge for integration into the existing system and considering past performance on similar projects is essential; hence a significant portion of the evaluation will be based on experience to ensure reliability and efficiency

The capability score reflects our need for robust, reliable equipment that can handle complex tasks with minimal downtime. A higher weight ensures we select suppliers who are not only experienced but also technically capable of delivering the required performance standards

Adegya's financial stability and management skills will be crucial for handling potential cost overruns or unforeseen expenses, hence a moderate weight is assigned to this criterion. It ensures that we can rely on their ability to manage the project effectively

Given our commitment to maintaining high-quality standards and minimizing disru0125 7e9rsees, quality management holds a significant weight in selection as it directly impacts system reliability. A higher score here indicates better adherence to industry best practices
Workplace Health & Safety is important but given the lower risk factors associated with this project and its non-critical nature compared to others, we assign a slightly lesser weight

evaluation_methodology.overview: The Evaluation Committee, in accordance with the evaluation methodology specified in the RFT and reproduced in this document, will evaluate all tenders. Compliance is generally taken to mean submission of the Tender by the closing date, in accordance with all lodgement instructions and provision of all information requested in the RFT (including any late tender provisions). Compliance criteria are not scored or weighted; depending on the nature, extent and implications of partial or non-compliance, a Tender may be disqualified from further evaluation.

compliance_criteria_table.title: 6.1.1 Compliance Criteria
notes: Compliance criteria are not scored or weighted. Non-compliant tenders may be excluded from further evaluation.

non_price_rating_scale.label: Exceptional
non_price_rating_scale.description: Fully compliant; no risks or weaknesses.

non_price_rating_scale.label: Superior
non_price_rating_scale.description: Sound achievement; minor risks/weaknesses may be acceptable.

non_price_rating_scale.label: Good
non_price_rating_scale.description: Reasonable achievement; errors/risks can be corrected with minimal effort.

non_price_rating_scale.label: Adequate
non_price_rating_scale.description: Minimal achievement; issues possible to correct/overcome.

returnable_scoring.note: See evaluation workbook and schedules for detailed rubrics.

pricing.pricing_model_text:
Pricing for tender evaluation will be normalised; the lowest fee achieves the maximum score (equal to the price weighting). State Government method:
Ps = 200 – (Pc / Pav × 100)
Pn = Ps / 200
Pw = Pn × [Price Weight]
Pc = Total Net Cost, Pav = Average of Total Net Costs.

local_preference_policy.blacktown_lga_discount: 5% discount capped at $50,000 for suppliers located in Blacktown City LGA
local_preference_policy.western_sydney_discount: 2.5% discount capped at $25,000 for suppliers located in Western Sydney councils
definitions: Blacktown City Local supplier: principal place of business (not a PO Box) located within Blacktown City LGA.
Western Sydney Council supplier: principal place of business (not a PO Box) located within a Western Sydney Council LGA.
local_preference_policy.application_note: For any suppliers eligible for local preference, provide the confirming address in the third tab of the tender evaluation spreadsheet.

value_for_money: Assessed on qualitative outcomes + price: quality and fitness for purpose vs whole-of-life costs vs risk.
Consider whole-of-life costs incl. maintenance and warranty obligations
Energy efficiency and control strategy for chillers/AHUs
‘Value for Money’ is assessed on the combined outcomes of qualitative criteria and price.
Consider quality of proposed services against whole-of-life costs and risk (capacity to deliver on-time and on-budget).
pricing.interviews_and_presentation_criteria: Scores may be revised following interviews/presentations of shortlisted tenderers.

model.narrative: Pricing for tender evaluation will be normalised; the lowest fee achieves the maximum score equal to the Price criterion weighting. In accordance with State Government scoring, the Total Net Cost for tender evaluation is normalised and weighted for comparison as follows.
variables: Pc = Total Net Cost calculated for tender evaluation
Pav = Average of all Total Net Costs
notes: The total score each Tenderer receives provides a numeric basis for comparison.
Recommendation on the preferred Tender will be based on scoring comparisons.
The basis of any increase will be calculated for the full term of the contract, including optional extensions.

tepp.contract_negotiations: A period of negotiation with the successful Tenderer may arise following completion of the Tender phase and will commence subject to approval by the Director. This period will result in the executed copies of the contractual documentation being completed and will necessitate a combination of meetings comprising formal written obligations to be resolved. The role of the negotiators is to ensure that the negotiation approach is appropriate to the nature of the project, is open and fair, meets the needs of the Tenderer and can be accommodated within the Tenderer’s resources. Negotiations must not result in material changes to the requirements of the Tender. The outcome of the negotiations will be reflected in the final contract and all negotiation discussions and outcomes will be documented.
tepp.debriefing: The Evaluation Panel may arrange for the debriefing of any unsuccessful Tenderers who request such a debriefing. The purpose of the debriefing is to provide Tenderers with general comments regarding evaluation of the Tenderer’s submission against the criteria. If undertaken, the debriefing process will be conducted by at least two members of the Evaluation Committee and may be carried out by telephone or letter. The debriefing process will be limited to the unsuccessful Tenderer’s offer. No comparisons will be made with the successful Tender and the debriefing process will not be used to justify the selection of the successful Tender. No scoring details will be provided.
tepp.contract_management: The contract will be managed by Manager City Fleet or delegate.
critical_dates: Commencement date in spec (01 Aug 2025) is earlier than policy minimum; adjusted to 09 Oct 2025 (≥ title date + 26 days).
how_to_complete_tepp: 1) Title Page — Replace Tender Number, Name and Term.
2) 1. Aim — Update Tender Name and Term.
3) 2. Description of Requirement — Replace with contract description and main requirements.
4) 2.1 Desired Outcomes — Insert procurement outcomes (value for money, timely delivery, etc.).
5) 2.2 Purpose of RFT — Replace with reasons for this tender (or EOI/Quotation).
… (continue your internal guidance list as needed) …
Update footer to correct Tender Number and Tender Name.

summary: The BLCS Main Pool Hall Heating & Ventilation - AHU 1 & 3 Refurbishments involve the comprehensive upgrade of existing heating, ventilation and air conditioning systems within a leisure centre's main pool hall. This includes replacing outdated equipment with new reverse cycle water-cooled heat pump chillers to enhance energy efficiency.

The refurbishment also encompasses the installation of additional plant room external wall louvers for better access during delivery and retrieval processes, as well as making good any disturbances caused by these activities.
Scope includes: Replacement/upgrade of water-cooled heat pump chillers; AHU coil refurbishments; 8 EC plug fans; BMCS integration (e.g., BACnet HLI)

tepp.shortlisting: Clearly non-competitive tenders may be excluded with reasons recorded.
alternative_tenders: Alternative tenders will be evaluated in accordance with the RFT evaluation methodology reproduced in this document.
tenderer_presentations: Tenderers may be invited to interview to clarify their tender. No new or additional information will be requested or permitted at this stage (see Section 3.8 – Requests for Clarification). Answers must be factual and unbiased; no personal opinions are to be expressed.
evaluation_report: On completion of the evaluation, results will be documented in a Council Report including:
• a summary of the evaluation method,
• the recommendation of the preferred tender, and
• the rationale used to select the preferred supplier.

The report will highlight key issues for negotiation/ongoing scrutiny, be considered by the Tender Review Committee, and, if approved, be endorsed by all Committee Members and presented to the Ordinary Council Meeting for adoption. The ultimate decision maker is Blacktown City Council’s elected officials.
tepp.debriefing_unsuccessful: Debriefing may be offered upon request. Only general comments on the tenderer’s own submission; no comparisons; no scoring details.

compliance_criteria: Comply with SafeWork NSW / Council WHS
Provide PPE for all on-site workers
Provide commissioning plan and evidence of tests per Section 10

required_criteria.name: Methodology
required_criteria.description: Commissioning plan covering: 10.1 General; Provide all equipment, labour, materials, power, consumables, etc. to conduct testing and; commissioning. The Contractor shall carry out his own tests to verify operation and performance; before requesting Council to attend inspections. Submit fully detailed Test Log sheets for inspection; prior to any testing taking place. The Test Log sheets shall be A4 in size and present a clear and; precise record of all tests to be carried out.…; BMCS integration/HLI (BACnet) with IO schedules and point lists; Program includes defects liability / warranty of 12 months; Provision for service/maintenance handover & schedules

required_criteria.name: Quality
required_criteria.description: Quality plan incl. ITPs; as-built docs; performance verification

required_criteria.name: Work Health & Safety
required_criteria.description: Comply with SafeWork NSW / Council WHS; Provide PPE for all on-site workers; Electrical work to AS/NZS 3000

interviews_presentations: Final scores may be revised following interviews/presentations conducted with shortlisted Tenderers, in accordance with Section 6.4 Tenderer Presentations.
shortlisting_alternatives: Where many tenders are received, clearly non-competitive tenders with no reasonable prospect of best value may be excluded from detailed evaluation. Reasons for exclusion must be defensible and recorded in the evaluation report.

instructions: Use the provided templates to complete the required Schedules.
Provide the requested information using the DOCX file and submit as a PDF.
If a schedule does not have enough space, include the information in a separate attachment and reference it in the schedule.
Unless otherwise stated, all documents (schedules and supporting documents) must be submitted in PDF format.
Prices in Schedule 10 must be clear and unambiguous; replace zero/blank with 'included' or 'excluded' and provide an explanation if necessary. Council may reject tenders with ambiguous pricing.
supporting_information: Capacity, including past performance and resources
Management & financial capacity (including financial statements for the last 3 years)
Quality management (policy, management plan, forms and checklists)
WHS management (completed Council WHS forms, WHS policy & plan, safe work method statements, checklists and forms)
Copies of certificates for required third‑party accreditations and insurances
Equal employment opportunity and fair employment evidence
Any additional relevant information that may assist in assessing the tender

supporting_documents: Quality management plans and inspection test plans
WHS manuals, policies, sample checklists and forms

sections.heading: Company Details
fields: Entity Type (e.g., Pty Ltd, Partnership, Sole Trader)

sections.heading: Declaration
sections.content: I/We declare that we have read and understood the Conditions of Tendering and agree to be bound by them, that we are solvent and not under any form of external administration, that we have disclosed any conflicts of interest, that we hold or will obtain the required insurances and licences, and that this tender constitutes a firm offer for the period specified.

sections.heading: Financial Statements
sections.content: Provide annual financial statements (balance sheet and profit/loss) for the last three years. If statements cannot be provided, explain why and supply alternative evidence of financial capacity.

sections.heading: Confidentiality
sections.content: Acknowledge that all information provided by Council is confidential and must not be disclosed except as permitted.

sections.heading: Conflict of Interest
sections.content: Disclose any actual, potential or perceived conflicts of interest that may influence your ability to perform the contract. Include details of the conflict and the proposed mitigation strategy.

sections.heading: ICAC Guidance
sections.content: Refer to NSW ICAC guidance on managing conflicts of interest in procurement for further information.

sections.heading: Supporting Documents
sections.content: Attach curriculum vitae and/or licences evidencing qualifications and experience for each proposed key personnel.

rows: Workers’ Compensation (as required under NSW law)

sections.heading: Subcontractor Insurances
sections.content: List insurances held by all subcontractors. Tenderers may be required to increase cover limits if Council deems necessary.

sections.heading: Relevant Projects
sections.content: Provide details of at least three relevant projects completed in the last five years, including client name, project description, contract value, start and finish dates, and your scope of work.

sections.heading: Councils Serviced
sections.content: List any councils or local government organisations you are currently providing services to and the number of years you have been engaged.

sections.heading: Client Referees
sections.content: Provide details for at least two senior client referees (up to four may be provided).

sections.heading: Methodology & Resources
sections.content: Describe your methodology for delivering the works, including sequencing of activities, resource allocations (personnel, plant, equipment, suppliers and subcontractors), and how you will ensure quality outcomes.

sections.heading: Quality & Accreditations
sections.content: Outline your quality management system, including reference to any third‑party accreditations and inspection test plans (ITPs). Attach sample reports where available.

sections.heading: Milestones

rows: Delivery and installation of major equipment
Handover and defects liability period commencement

sections.content: Populate the milestones table with the key deliverables for this project. If Council has mandated a fixed date for commencement or completion (e.g., start of operations by TBA), ensure your program aligns with that date.

sections.heading: Disruption Minimisation
sections.content: Explain how you will minimise disruption to Council operations and stakeholders during the works.

sections.heading: Q&A Confirmations
sections.content: Confirm that you will provide the following information as part of your tender or prior to award: product catalogues/technical sheets, draft layout diagrams/drawings, crew qualifications and experience, warranty terms, equipment origin (country of manufacture), and details of any proposed subcontractors.

sections.heading: Performance / Technical Specification
sections.content: List the key performance requirements and technical specifications for the major equipment or services to be supplied. For example, device capacities, ratings, compliance standards, control interfaces, and any basis‑of‑design references.

sections.heading: Pricing Table
sections.content: Provide a lump sum price in Australian dollars for each scope package. Pricing must include GST, levies, duties, overheads and all other costs necessary to perform the works. Prices must be valid for the tender validity period.

sections.heading: CSR Policy / Initiatives
sections.content: Describe your organisation’s corporate social responsibility policy or a relevant CSR initiative.

sections.heading: Sustainability Practices
sections.content: Explain your organisation’s understanding of sustainability and outline the practices or initiatives you have in place to support sustainable procurement and operations.

sections.heading: Policies & Evidence
sections.content: Provide copies of your organisation’s policies and evidence of practices relating to equal employment opportunity and fair employment. This may include recruitment and training policies, anti‑discrimination and harassment policies, consultation and dispute resolution processes.

sections.heading: Industrial Relations & Commitments
sections.content: State any past breaches of wage or superannuation obligations and confirm rectification. Provide your commitments to employing Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, apprentices and trainees, and your acknowledgement of the Industrial Relations Undertaking.

sections.heading: Acknowledgement of Council WHS Policy
sections.content: Acknowledge that you have read Council’s WHS Policy and will comply with all WHS obligations applicable to the contract.

sections.heading: Required WHS Evidence
sections.content: Provide copies of your WHS manuals, policies and procedures; descriptions of WHS responsibilities and consultation processes; sample checklists and inspection forms; records of WHS incidents and corrective actions; training records; risk assessments/SWMS; registers and SDS; plant and equipment maintenance records; permit‑to‑work samples; and a site‑specific WHS plan for a similar project.

sections.heading: Chain of Responsibility (CoR)
sections.content: If transport of goods is involved, provide evidence of your Chain of Responsibility compliance, including a CoR policy, risk register, KPIs, training records, fleet maintenance and diary records, and subcontractor pre‑engagement audits.

sections.heading: Required WHS Forms
sections.content: Attach the completed WHS Forms (Appendices 1–3) and WHS Policy (Appendix 4).

sections.heading: Supplier Declarations
sections.content: Provide details of whether your organisation is reporting under the Australian Modern Slavery Act. Attach any modern slavery policies and describe your due diligence processes, training initiatives and supply chain risk assessments. List the countries of manufacture for products or components relevant to this tender.

sections.heading: Addenda Acknowledgement
sections.content: List all addenda issued for this RFT and indicate acknowledgment.

sections.heading: Declaration
sections.content: I/We declare that we have received all addenda listed above, that our tender complies with all requirements of the tender documents except as noted, that the information provided in all returnable schedules is true and correct, and that our offer remains open for the tender validity period (e.g., 120 days or as specified).

spec_summary.title: BLCS Main Pool Hall Heating & Ventilation - AHU 1 & 3 Refurbishments
scope: Main Pool Hall Heating & Ventilation - AHU 1 & 3 Refurbishments
Proprietary metallic‐coated steel channel section with clamps and hangers sized match external diameter of pipe being supported.
Fit moulded plastic end caps to all channels.
Vertical pipes: Provide anchors and guides to maintain long pipes in position and supports to balance the mass of the pipe and its contents.
Change in Direction: Provide a support at each change of direction.

tests: 10.1 General
Provide all equipment, labour, materials, power, consumables, etc. to conduct testing and
commissioning. The Contractor shall carry out his own tests to verify operation and performance
before requesting Council to attend inspections. Submit fully detailed Test Log sheets for inspection
prior to any testing taking place. The Test Log sheets shall be A4 in size and present a clear and
precise record of all tests to be carried out.
10.2 Testing
The following tests and checks shall be carried out by the Contractor:
1. Visual inspection of pipework and joints for leaks.
2. Water-test drains and overflow piping for leaks and for unobstructed drainage.
3. Check all power cabling terminations and megger test each circuit.
4. Verify integrity of all controls cabling to ensure electrical continuity and freedom from shorts and
grounds.
5. Sequence checks of all specified control functions and cycles, such as Start, Stop, Chiller Staging,
Cooling and Heating Cycles, etc.
6. Water flow rates shall be balanced to +5% / -0% of those scheduled. Measure and record Chilled
Water flow rates, compare with scheduled design water flows, and submit.
Make all necessary adjustments and record all temperature set points and differentials on the Test
Log sheets.
BLCS Main Pool Hall Heating & Ventilation - AHU 1 & 3 Refurbishments                                                                     Page 19 of 20
maintenance: The Installation Contractor is responsible for carrying out all preventative maintenance and servicing
for the duration of the Defects Liability Period.
The mechanical contractor shall allow in the tender for maintenance and servicing from date of
practical completion to the end of the maintenance and defects liability period, to comply with
requirements of AS 3666, Local Authority and any other statutory body having jurisdiction.
Maintenance, in addition to rectification of faults, emergency service and carrying out of capacity
tests as required, shall also include a minimum of 12 regular monthly visits for routine maintenance.
whs: Comply with SafeWork NSW / Council WHS
Provide PPE for all on-site workers

decommission: 1. Removal and disposal of two (2) existing belt driven exhaust fans.
2. Removal and disposal of the two (2) sets of air-to-air heat exchangers
3. Removal and disposal of the exhaust plenum floors and replace with non-skid aluminium plates
19. Associated builder’s work, making good, removal of redundant items and construction rubbish,
The plantroom external wall louvres shall be removed temporarily to allow removal of the larger
When dispatching plant or materials to site, or removal of plant or materials from site, the Contractor
wiring, etc. Plastic components that cannot be recycled shall be disposed of in accordance with
Modify the supply and exhaust chambers to suit new equipment to be installed and as follows:
a. Remove and dispose existing filter bags. Frames and tracks may be re-used.
b. Re-instate filter frames into the new outside air intake centre compartment to fit 8-off 600x600
bag air filters, and then, supply and install new 600x600, 600mm deep bag air filters.
c. Remove and dispose off-site the following items within the section of AHU 1 & 3 to give way
for the repair and modification works:
1) Air-to-air heat exchanger, frames, condensate sump and drainpipes
2) Badly corroded exhaust air fan, discharge duct up to the joint below roof and electricals
3) Front external panels and filter frames, including filter frames on plenum roofs
4) All redundant items and other items not required in the proposed modification.
d. Sand to remove (minor) rust, rust treat surface and coat with paint as necessary
e. Install new minimum 2.0mm or thicker aluminium checkered plates over the existing discharge
plenum floors.
f. Remove section of sheet metal ductwork above AHUs to allow connection of the new exhaust
discharge configuration.
g. Supply and install internally-insulated galvanised sheet metal ductwork above AHUs to
connect new exhaust plenum to existing exhaust ductwork
h. Re-enforce existing galvanised sheet metal partition between AHUs to withstand exhaust air
pressure
i.
Patch up gaps with galvanised sheet metal between coils and walls to minimise air leakages
j.
Supply and install new large lockable access panel for service access to each AHUs
k. Clean, repaint and re-instate existing AHU access doors. Council to install safety warnings on
the access doors.
l.
All physical dimensions and suitability to be confirmed on site before manufacture.
BLCS Main Pool Hall Heating & Ventilation - AHU 1 & 3 Refurbishments                                                                     Page 8 of 20
mechanical: a. Design, supply and install condenser and evaporator water coils including insulated
pipework, corrosion-resistant sump, drain pipes, supports, fittings, etc. Chlorine resistant
coating shall be applied to coil fins.
b. Design, supply and install heat recovery coils including recirculating pumps, pipework,
corrosion-resistant sump, drain pipes, supports, fittings, etc. Chlorine resistant coating shall
be applied to coil fins.
c.  Supply, deliver and install new EC fans (4-off) in each AHUs at 2,800 l/s capacity each to
replace the badly-corroded centrifugal exhaust fans, including building up of adequate fan
supports and re-enforced exhaust plenums
d. Design, supply, deliver and install finned coils in the fresh air and exhaust air plenums sized
based on the proposed heat pump chiller performance
e. Supply and install aluminium drift eliminators upstream of the evaporator coils
f. Supply and install condensate sumps common to the cool side of the heat recovery coils,
evaporator coils including the drift eliminators.
g. Interconnecting pipeworks into each system including valves, fittings and other essential
items associated works such as bleeds, drains, etc
h. Supply and install all associated and interconnecting pipework, make-up water/RPZ valve,
chemical dosing, closed-type expansion tank valves, fittings, spring/rubber isolation, gages
cocks, installation of thermowells (supplied by others), etc to form a complete and
operational chilled/condenser water system. All pipework and electrical cabling/trunking to
run 2.1m min from floor in trafficable area.
i.
Supply and install 80mm table B copper chilled and condenser water pipework with
necessary valves, brackets and fittings from new headers to connect 3x coils in each AHUs
j.
Fill and pressure test pipe works using minimum 3x the system pressure.
k. Insulate chilled water pipework using foil faced foam shield type pipe insulation and metal
sheath at low level pipework (under 1.8m).

b. Disconnect power supply cables from the main switchboard into the MSSB-1 and reconnect
the power supply cables into the new MSSB-2(old MCC1).
c. Supply and install adequately sized power cables from the chiller sub-board located in the
Main Switch room into existing MSSB-1 located in Plantroom 1.
d. Supply and install switchgears in MSSB-1 to accommodate new chillers and provide power
to the new sub-board, including terminals and relays for BMS interfacing, ensuring correct
control and status on the Control.
e. Supply and install new electrical sub-board, cables and circuit breakers for the auxiliary
equipment including heat recovery circulation pumps and EC fans as shown on the drawings.
f. Supply and install cable tray, cable supports, cabling, isolator and connection where required
g. Supply and installation of cable support, cabling, isolator and connection to (8) Exhaust Fans
in the AHUs and the new chillers.

i. All other associated and necessary electrical works not specifically mentioned above
